name: add-gchat
tagline: Adds a Google Chat channel adapter to NanoClaw: copies code from a branch, registers it, installs a pinned dependency, builds/tests, and.
verdict: Solid mechanics: idempotent directives, an exact-pinned dependency, a real build+test gate, and a thorough troubleshooting section. Gaps: no 'not for' guidance so it can misfire against sibling per-channel skills, no version/changelog signal, pnpm/vitest usage is never declared or checked, and the branch-copy step calls unreviewed code 'canonical' before.

highlights.summary: Copies adapter code from the 'channels' branch with 'overwrite — the branch is canonical' — no review step before it's built and registered.
Description says what it does ('Add Google Chat channel integration via Chat SDK') but never states a triggering situation or phrasing.
No 'NOT for' / exclusion text anywhere, despite being one of an implied family of per-channel add-X skills (add-slack, etc.) with real misfire risk.
